chapter_title.ar: باب الْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا الْمَدْخُولِ بِهَا أَيْنَ تَعْتَدُّ وَ مَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 45 – The woman widowed from her husband who has been slept with, where should she wait from and what is Obligated upon her

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/201)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/201)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الَّتِي تُوُفِّيَ عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا أَ تَحُجُّ قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ تَخْرُجُ وَ تَنْتَقِلُ مِنْ مَنْزِلٍ إِلَى مَنْزِلٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazal, from Ibn Bukeyr who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the woman who is widowed from her husband, can she perform Hajj?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, and go out, and move from a house to a house’.
en.sarwar: 12. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Khalid from al-Qasim ibn ‘Urwah from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A widow whose husband has died must not use perfumes and beautify herself until her ‘Iddah (waiting period) of four months and ten days is complete.’”
